bio: Black nationalist leader and minister of the Nation of Islam; assassinated in 1965.
name: Malcolm X
top_work: The Autobiography of Malcolm X

bio: Baptist minister and leader of the American civil rights movement; Nobel Peace Prize laureate.
name: Martin Luther King Jr.
top_work: Why We Can't Wait

bio: Pulitzer Prize-winning journalist and author of works on the Great Migration and caste.
name: Isabel Wilkerson
top_work: The Warmth of Other Suns

bio: Civil rights lawyer, legal scholar, and writer on race and the criminal justice system.
name: Michelle Alexander
top_work: The New Jim Crow

bio: Oral historian and broadcaster known for collecting the testimonies of ordinary Americans.
name: Studs Terkel
top_work: Working

bio: Historian and Pulitzer Prize-winning author of the America in the King Years trilogy.
name: Taylor Branch
top_work: Parting the Waters: America in the King Years 1954-63

bio: Journalist and author who reported on labor, the working poor, and economic inequality.
name: Barbara Ehrenreich
top_work: Nickel and Dimed: On (Not) Getting By in America

bio: Sociologist, historian, and co-founder of the NAACP; a foundational scholar of Black American life.
name: W. E. B. Du Bois
top_work: The Souls of Black Folk

bio: Historian of American labor, lynching, and the long Black freedom struggle.
name: Philip Dray
top_work: There Is Power in a Union

bio: Detroit-born journalist and author known for gritty reporting on his home city.
name: Charlie LeDuff
top_work: Detroit: An American Autopsy

bio: Labor lawyer and writer chronicling the decline and meaning of the American labor movement.
name: Thomas Geoghegan
top_work: Which Side Are You On?: Trying to Be for Labor When It's Flat on Its Back

bio: Historian of Black radicalism, labor, and African American social movements.
name: Robin D. G. Kelley
top_work: Hammer and Hoe: Alabama Communists During the Great Depression

bio: Historian of race, inequality, and urban America, especially postwar Detroit.
name: Thomas J. Sugrue
top_work: The Origins of the Urban Crisis: Race and Inequality in Postwar Detroit

bio: Writer and historian who chronicled Detroit's radical labor and Black power movements.
name: Dan Georgakas
top_work: Detroit: I Do Mind Dying

bio: Theologian and founder of Black liberation theology.
name: James H. Cone
top_work: The Cross and the Lynching Tree

bio: Pulitzer Prize-winning historian and biographer of Martin Luther King Jr.
name: David J. Garrow
top_work: Bearing the Cross: Martin Luther King, Jr., and the Southern Christian Leadership Conference

bio: Historian and biographer of figures from the American Civil War and civil rights eras.
name: Stephen B. Oates
top_work: Let the Trumpet Sound: A Life of Martin Luther King, Jr.

bio: Historian, theologian, and aide to Martin Luther King Jr. in the freedom movement.
name: Vincent Harding
top_work: There Is a River: The Black Struggle for Freedom in America

bio: Historian, playwright, and activist known for history told from below.
name: Howard Zinn
top_work: A People's History of the United States

bio: Cultural historian of Detroit, Motown, and African American life.
name: Suzanne E. Smith
top_work: Dancing in the Street: Motown and the Cultural Politics of Detroit

bio: Theologian, mystic, and minister whose work shaped the civil rights movement.
name: Howard Thurman
top_work: Jesus and the Disinherited

bio: The Queen of Gospel; one of the most influential gospel singers of the twentieth century.
name: Mahalia Jackson
top_work: Movin' On Up

bio: Historian of American labor, class, and politics in the twentieth century.
name: Jefferson Cowie
top_work: Stayin' Alive: The 1970s and the Last Days of the Working Class
